title: Gandasulium hasseltii
intro: There is not enough information about Gandasulium hasseltii in the current knowledge database.
taxonomy: Gandasulium hasseltii is a species of plant in the family Myrsinaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and is a type of flowering plant. The classification of Gandasulium hasseltii includes its kingdom as Plantae and its phylum as Magnoliophyta. Further classification of the plant places it in the class Magnoliopsida and order Ericales. At the family and genus level, Gandasulium hasseltii is classified under the family Myrsinaceae and genus Gandasulium.
uses: Gandasulium hasseltii has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine. The plant's leaves and stems are used in various remedies, including treatments for fever and skin conditions. In some traditional practices, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to alleviate pain and swelling. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these uses. Its potential medicinal benefits make it an area of interest for further scientific study.
